content.question: What is the independent causal contribution of the 6/8-year track itself to Hungary's high between-school segregation, distinct from other structural mechanisms (free school choice, neighborhood segregation, school autonomy in admissions)?
content.reduction_path: Decomposition analysis isolating selection effects of the 6/8-track from other selection drivers through regression discontinuity or matched comparison of students marginally above/below selection cutoffs; comparative case study of Hungarian municipalities that have changed access rules or admissions criteria while keeping the track structure constant
